outliers.title: Advertising, promotions, and marketing managers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While digital tasks like working with computers and data-driven planning are highly susceptible to AI augmentation, the core value of the role lies in high-level interpersonal relationships and complex decision-making. The high importance of human-edge activities like influencing others and creative thinking provides a significant buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and information-based, involving market research, data analysis, and content strategy—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While high-level strategic decision-making and stakeholder management provide some insulation, AI can now automate significant portions of campaign planning, creative drafting, and consumer data processing, leading to major restructuring of the role.

outliers.title: Sales managers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with data-driven decision-making and communication drafting, the core high-importance activities like influencing others, building teams, and interpersonal relationship management require high-level human emotional intelligence. The role is heavily centered on real-time human judgment and social dynamics that current AI cannot replicate.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Sales managers perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, sales forecasting, and strategic planning, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation. While the role requires significant interpersonal leadership and relationship management, much of the core digital workflow—such as monitoring CRM data, generating reports, and developing training materials—is being rapidly transformed by generative and analytical AI.

outliers.title: Public relations and fundraising managers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with 'Getting Information', 'Working with Computers', and data-driven donor research, the core value of the role lies in high-stakes interpersonal relationships and influencing others. The necessity for real-time human judgment and emotional intelligence in fundraising mitigates the risk of large-scale displacement.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core digital tasks of this role—writing press releases, drafting speeches, analyzing social media trends, and creating fundraising strategies—are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. However, the role's heavy reliance on high-stakes interpersonal relationships, such as face-to-face donor meetings and managing media crises, provides a significant human-centric buffer that prevents a higher score.

outliers.title: Training and development managers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with 'Working with Computers' and 'Getting Information' through content generation and data analysis, the core high-importance activities like 'Training and Teaching Others' and 'Coaching and Developing Others' require high levels of emotional intelligence and real-time human judgment. The role is heavily interpersonal and relies on building relationships, which are difficult for AI to replicate effectively.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This role is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the creation of instructional content, data-driven needs assessments, and budget management—all areas where AI is highly capable. While the role requires significant human-centric leadership and stakeholder collaboration, AI can automate the generation of training materials, personalize learning paths, and analyze program effectiveness, substantially increasing the productivity of each manager.

outliers.title: Postsecondary education administrators
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with information gathering and organizational planning, the core functions of this role rely heavily on high-level decision-making, interpersonal relationship management, and motivating subordinates. The high importance of human-edge activities like creative thinking and team building provides a significant buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is heavily centered on digital information processing, including reviewing applications, analyzing student data, managing budgets, and maintaining academic records—all tasks where AI can significantly increase productivity or automate routine workflows. While roles like deans and student affairs officers require high-level human judgment and interpersonal relationship management, the underlying administrative and analytical work is highly susceptible to AI integration and restructuring.

outliers.title: Labor relations specialists
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with 'Getting Information' and drafting communications, the core functions rely on high-stakes negotiation, real-time conflict resolution, and complex interpersonal relationship management. These activities require nuanced human judgment and emotional intelligence that current AI cannot replicate in high-pressure labor environments.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is predominantly digital and knowledge-based, involving the drafting of contracts, policy development, and legal compliance—all areas where AI excels. While the high-stakes interpersonal negotiation and dispute resolution provide a buffer, AI will significantly automate the research, document generation, and investigative analysis components of the role.

outliers.title: Fundraisers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can automate data gathering and strategic planning, the core value of fundraising lies in high-stakes interpersonal relationships and influencing others, which require human empathy and trust. The occupation is heavily weighted toward social intelligence and real-time human judgment that current AI cannot replicate.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Fundraising involves significant digital work including donor research, data analysis, and the creation of promotional messaging, all of which are highly susceptible to AI automation and enhancement. While major gift solicitation and event management require high-level interpersonal skills and physical presence, the majority of the administrative, analytical, and communication-based tasks are moving toward AI-driven workflows.

outliers.title: Training and development specialists
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with updating knowledge and creating training content, the core high-importance activities like coaching, teaching, and maintaining interpersonal relationships require high levels of emotional intelligence and real-time human judgment. The role is heavily centered on interpersonal dynamics and social intelligence, which are difficult for current AI to replicate in a professional development context.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Much of the core work, such as designing training manuals, creating online learning modules, and assessing needs through data, is digital and highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the role requires interpersonal skills for live delivery and collaboration, AI can now generate curriculum, simulate training scenarios, and personalize learning paths, significantly increasing individual productivity and potentially reducing the number of specialists needed for content creation.

outliers.title: Computer and information research scientists
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly automate data analysis and information retrieval, the core value of this role lies in high-level creative problem solving and decision-making. The high importance of human-edge activities like creative thinking and interpersonal relationship management provides a strong buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is fundamentally digital, involving high-level coding, algorithm design, and data analysis—all areas where AI is rapidly advancing. While these scientists are the ones building AI, the tools they create are increasingly capable of automating their own core tasks, such as writing code, simplifying algorithms, and analyzing experimental results, leading to extreme productivity gains and role restructuring.

outliers.title: Computer network architects
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can automate technical tasks like network configuration, information gathering, and troubleshooting data, the core of the role relies on high-level decision-making and complex problem-solving. The high importance of human-edge activities like creative thinking and interpersonal coordination suggests that AI will act more as a productivity enhancer than a replacement.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core work of designing, documenting, and configuring networks is fundamentally digital and data-driven, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and optimization tools. While physical hardware deployment and complex stakeholder management provide a slight buffer, AI is rapidly advancing in automated network topology design, security configuration, and predictive troubleshooting, which will significantly increase individual productivity and restructure the role.

outliers.title: Network and computer systems administrators
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can automate routine network monitoring, information gathering, and basic troubleshooting, the role relies heavily on high-level decision-making and complex problem-solving. The human-edge activities like creative thinking and interpersonal relationship management provide a significant buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and involves tasks like configuration, monitoring, and troubleshooting that are highly susceptible to AI-driven automation and 'infrastructure as code' practices. While some physical hardware maintenance remains, the shift toward cloud computing and AI-powered network management tools is already leading to a projected decline in employment as fewer workers can manage larger, more complex systems.

outliers.title: Mathematicians and statisticians
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with analyzing data, processing information, and working with computers, the core value of a mathematician lies in creative thinking and complex problem-solving. The high importance of human-edge activities like training, teaching, and decision-making provides a strong buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital, involving data analysis, mathematical modeling, and coding—all domains where AI and Large Language Models excel. While high-level theoretical research and complex interdisciplinary communication require human judgment, AI can now automate significant portions of data cleaning, statistical testing, and code generation, drastically increasing individual productivity and restructuring the workflow.

outliers.title: Computer hardware engineers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with analyzing data, getting information, and updating technical knowledge, the core functions of hardware engineering require physical interaction with components and complex real-time problem-solving. The high importance of creative thinking and decision-making provides a strong buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core work of designing schematics, analyzing circuits, and testing components is increasingly performed using digital Electronic Design Automation (EDA) tools that are highly susceptible to AI-driven optimization and automation. While there is a physical component to testing and manufacturing oversight, the vast majority of the engineering lifecycle is digital and benefits significantly from AI's ability to optimize complex systems and generate code/hardware descriptions.

outliers.title: Medical scientists
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with 'Getting Information' and 'Updating and Using Relevant Knowledge' through data synthesis, the core value of this role lies in 'Thinking Creatively' and 'Making Decisions and Solving Problems' within complex biological contexts. The high educational requirement and the need for high-level human judgment in scientific discovery suggest that AI will act as a powerful co-pilot rather than a replacement.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Medical scientists perform high-level knowledge work including data analysis, grant writing, and literature reviews, all of which are highly susceptible to AI augmentation and automation. While physical laboratory work and clinical trials provide a buffer, the core of the profession is shifting toward computational biology and AI-driven drug discovery, significantly increasing productivity and reshaping research methodologies.

outliers.title: Psychologists
outliers.gemma_rationale: The core functions of this role rely on high-level interpersonal relationships, empathy, and real-time human judgment which are difficult for AI to replicate. While AI can assist with documenting information and retrieving data, the primary clinical decision-making and therapeutic interventions remain human-essential.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Psychology is a knowledge-based profession where core tasks like analyzing data, writing reports, and researching are highly susceptible to AI enhancement. However, the critical requirements for human empathy, ethical judgment, and real-time interpersonal observation in clinical and school settings create a significant barrier to full automation.

outliers.title: Lawyers
outliers.gemma_rationale: AI can significantly assist with 'Getting Information' and 'Evaluating Information to Determine Compliance with Standards' through legal research and document review. However, high-importance tasks like 'Resolving Conflicts and Negotiating with Others' and 'Making Decisions' require complex human judgment and interpersonal nuance that current AI cannot replicate.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Lawyers perform high-level knowledge work that is almost entirely digital, involving document drafting, legal research, and complex data analysis—all areas where LLMs excel. While courtroom advocacy and high-stakes interpersonal negotiation provide a protective barrier, the vast majority of billable hours are spent on tasks that AI can now significantly accelerate or automate, leading to major structural changes in the profession.

outliers.title: Arbitrators, mediators, and conciliators
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with analyzing data and evaluating information for compliance, the core functions of resolving conflicts and negotiating require high-level emotional intelligence and real-time human judgment. The occupation is heavily reliant on interpersonal relationships and complex decision-making that current AI cannot replicate.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is primarily digital and information-based, involving the analysis of legal documents, evidence, and the drafting of settlement agreements—tasks where AI is rapidly achieving high proficiency. While the role requires significant interpersonal skills and emotional intelligence to manage confrontational parties, the core analytical and administrative functions are highly susceptible to AI-driven productivity gains and partial automation.

outliers.title: High school teachers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can automate lesson planning, grading, and administrative tasks, the core functions of the role rely on high-importance human-edge activities like coaching, interpersonal relationship building, and real-time classroom management. The necessity of physical presence and emotional intelligence to manage student behavior and social development provides a significant buffer against automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: High school teachers are heavily exposed because core digital tasks like lesson planning, content creation, and grading are highly susceptible to AI automation and augmentation. While the physical requirement of classroom management and interpersonal mentorship provides a buffer, AI's ability to provide personalized tutoring and automated feedback significantly reshapes the instructional delivery and administrative workload of the profession.

outliers.title: Adult basic and secondary education and ESL teachers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with lesson planning, content generation, and interpreting information, the core of this role relies on high-importance human-edge activities like coaching, interpersonal relationship building, and real-time teaching. The necessity of emotional intelligence and real-time human judgment in an educational setting significantly limits full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core work product—language instruction, literacy training, and test preparation—is fundamentally digital and information-based, making it highly susceptible to AI-driven tutoring and translation tools. While the role requires significant cultural sensitivity and interpersonal motivation, AI can now provide personalized lesson plans, real-time feedback, and adaptive learning paths that significantly increase teacher productivity or replace routine instructional tasks.

outliers.title: Tutors
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with lesson planning and content generation, the core work activities like coaching, interpersonal relationship building, and real-time problem solving require high levels of human empathy and social intelligence. The occupation relies heavily on human-centric soft skills that are difficult for current AI to replicate in a meaningful pedagogical context.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Tutors primarily engage in knowledge transfer, explanation, and feedback—tasks where generative AI and personalized learning algorithms excel. While the interpersonal and motivational aspects of tutoring provide some protection, the core work product is digital/informational, and AI's ability to provide 24/7, low-cost, personalized instruction for standardized tests and core subjects creates significant displacement risk.

outliers.title: Art directors
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with 'Working with Computers' and generating visual assets, the core 'Human-Edge' activities like creative direction, complex decision-making, and interpersonal negotiation remain difficult to automate. The high importance of 'Thinking Creatively' and 'Making Decisions' suggests AI will act more as a productivity multiplier than a replacement for the role.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Art directors work in a fundamentally digital domain where AI is rapidly advancing in image generation, layout design, and video synthesis. While the role requires high-level human judgment, leadership, and client communication, the core work product can be heavily augmented or automated by AI, significantly increasing productivity and potentially reducing the number of directors needed to oversee creative projects.

outliers.title: Actors
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with creative brainstorming and digital performance synthesis, the core work activities rely heavily on physical presence, real-time interpersonal relationships, and performing for the public. The high importance of physical activities and direct human interaction provides a significant buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: While live theater and physical presence on set remain human-centric, the occupation is highly exposed due to AI's ability to generate digital likenesses, synthesize voices, and replace background extras or voiceover artists. The core work product—audio and video—is increasingly susceptible to generative AI, leading to significant industry restructuring and potential displacement in digital media productions.

outliers.title: Public relations specialists
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with 'Getting Information' and drafting communications, the core value of the role lies in 'Establishing and Maintaining Interpersonal Relationships' and 'Thinking Creatively,' which require high emotional intelligence. The high importance of real-time human judgment and strategic decision-making mitigates the risk of full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core tasks of this occupation—writing press releases, drafting speeches, creating social media content, and monitoring public sentiment—are digital-first and align perfectly with the capabilities of Large Language Models. While high-level strategy and real-time interpersonal crisis management provide some protection, the vast majority of the daily work product can be generated or significantly optimized by AI, leading to high exposure and potential workforce consolidation.

outliers.title: Broadcast, sound, and video technicians
outliers.gemma_rationale: The role is primarily physical and requires real-time manual dexterity to install, adjust, and maintain lighting equipment in various environments. While AI could assist with lighting design simulations or automated scheduling, the core work requires physical presence and hands-on troubleshooting that cannot be digitized.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical labor (setting up hardware, rigging lights, running cables) and digital knowledge work (mixing sound, editing video, managing broadcast signals). While the physical installation tasks are resistant to AI, the digital components—such as sound leveling, video editing, and signal monitoring—are being rapidly automated by AI tools, leading to higher productivity and a projected decline in specialized roles like sound and broadcast technicians.

outliers.title: Film and video editors and camera operators
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with digital post-production and automated shot recognition, the core role requires physical presence for handling and moving objects and real-time creative decision-making on set. The high importance of interpersonal relationships and physical coordination makes the role resistant to full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is a hybrid of physical camera operation and digital video editing. While camera operators have a physical barrier to AI due to the need for on-site equipment management and real-world positioning, editors work entirely in digital environments where AI is rapidly automating tasks like color grading, sound syncing, and even rough-cut generation. The rise of generative video and AI-driven post-production tools significantly increases the productivity of each worker, potentially reducing the total number of editors needed for complex projects.

outliers.title: Medical dosimetrists
outliers.gemma_rationale: High importance on computer-based tasks like documenting information and processing data suggests significant AI augmentation potential for workflow efficiency. However, the critical need for complex decision-making, problem-solving, and clinical judgment provides a strong human-edge buffer against full automation.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core of this occupation involves complex mathematical calculations, image analysis (CT/MRI), and digital treatment planning, all of which are domains where AI and machine learning excel. While there is a minor physical component involving patient positioning and equipment calibration, the vast majority of the work is digital information processing that is highly susceptible to automation and AI-driven optimization.

outliers.title: Cashiers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI and automated kiosks can handle 'Getting Information' and transaction processing, the core 'Human-Edge' activities like direct public interaction and interpersonal relationship management require physical presence. The low exposure score reflects that the role's primary value lies in real-time human service and physical handling of objects which are difficult to automate fully.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: While the job has physical components like bagging and stocking, the core function of processing transactions is highly susceptible to automation through computer vision and AI-driven self-checkout systems. The BLS already projects a significant decline in employment due to these technological shifts, as AI can increasingly handle age verification, fraud detection, and customer inquiries.

outliers.title: Securities, commodities, and financial services sales agents
outliers.gemma_rationale: High-importance tasks like 'Working with Computers' and 'Processing Information' are highly susceptible to AI automation through algorithmic trading and data analysis. However, the core value of the role relies on 'Establishing and Maintaining Interpersonal Relationships' and 'Negotiating,' which require human trust and complex social judgment.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: This occupation is almost entirely digital and information-based, involving data analysis, market monitoring, and financial modeling—all areas where AI is rapidly achieving parity with human experts. While high-level relationship management and complex negotiation (like M&A) provide some insulation, the core tasks of identifying investment opportunities, executing trades, and providing routine financial advice are highly susceptible to automation and significant productivity gains that could reduce the need for human agents.

outliers.title: Travel agents
outliers.gemma_rationale: High-importance tasks like 'Working with Computers' and 'Getting Information' are highly susceptible to AI automation through automated booking and itinerary generation. However, the occupation relies heavily on human-edge activities like 'Establishing and Maintaining Interpersonal Relationships' and 'Resolving Conflicts,' which require high emotional intelligence.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core functions of a travel agent—researching destinations, comparing prices, building itineraries, and processing bookings—are entirely digital and involve information synthesis that AI can now perform instantly. While high-end human agents provide personalized judgment and crisis management, AI's ability to handle complex natural language queries and real-time logistics makes the vast majority of the occupation's routine tasks highly susceptible to automation.

outliers.title: Models
outliers.gemma_rationale: The core work activities involve physical presence, creative expression, and interpersonal interaction, which are difficult for current AI to replicate in a real-world setting. While AI can assist with scheduling or information gathering, the primary value of a model lies in physical performance and human aesthetics.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: While runway and live event modeling require physical presence, the majority of modeling work results in digital or print imagery which is highly vulnerable to AI-generated synthetic media. AI can now create hyper-realistic virtual models or 'digital twins' of existing models, allowing brands to showcase clothing and products without physical photo shoots, significantly reducing the demand for human labor in commercial and catalog sectors.

outliers.title: Sales engineers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can significantly assist with 'Getting Information' and 'Working with Computers,' the core value of a Sales Engineer lies in 'Selling or Influencing Others' and 'Establishing Interpersonal Relationships.' These human-edge activities require high-stakes social intelligence and real-time judgment that current AI cannot replicate in a complex B2B sales environment.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: Sales engineers perform high-level knowledge work that is increasingly digital, including technical presentations, product customization, and troubleshooting. While AI can automate technical documentation, demo generation, and data-driven customer insights, the role's heavy reliance on complex interpersonal relationships, negotiation, and trust-building provides a significant buffer against full automation.

outliers.title: Air traffic controllers
outliers.gemma_rationale: While AI can assist with monitoring processes and identifying objects, the core high-importance task of making critical real-time decisions requires human judgment to manage safety-critical variables. The occupation relies heavily on high-stakes problem solving and complex communication that current AI cannot autonomously manage in live airspace environments.
outliers.karpathy_rationale: The core work is fundamentally digital and data-driven, involving the monitoring of radar screens and the processing of complex spatial information to ensure aircraft separation. While the high-stakes nature of safety and the need for real-time human communication with pilots provide a buffer, AI and automated systems (like NextGen) are already increasing productivity and handling more traffic per controller, leading to a very low projected growth in human employment.
